inline_comments.message:
This file represent HEAT types, HEAT is not supporting
scalar-unit.size,  scalar-unit.time and scalar-unit.frequency, they are TOSCA types.
According to heat spec heat type can be ONLY one of the following 
<string | number | json | comma_delimited_list | boolean>
https://docs.openstack.org/heat/latest/template_guide/hot_spec.html#
see Parameters section

You can't change the heat file to be invalid
The translation which was done was from number to float
if you want to represent it differently in TOSCA you can but it should be valid translation, the input is number.
Please note that the size in heat OS::Cinder::Volume is define in GB, according to heat spec - https://docs.openstack.org/heat/latest/template_guide/openstack.html#OS::Cinder::Volume
so if you change the translation to tosca scalar-unit.size, the units should be aligned.
We define it in TOSCA as MB so this is why we added (*1024) to heat size in order to get TOSCA size
Maybe the default value in the TOSCA type can be changed to GB and then the (*1024) can be removed, I don't remember why it was set to be MB

Hi Shiri Amichai, thank you for your comments. As HEAT does not support scalar unit type, I understand that the translation should be done from number to scalar.unit.size and the default size value to GB, as according to TOSCA spec, the scalar-unit type can be used to define scalar values along with a unit from the list of recognized units - http://docs.oasis-open.org/tosca/TOSCA-Simple-Profile-YAML/v1.0/csprd01/TOSCA-Simple-Profile-YAML-v1.0-csprd01.html. If you agree with that I can proceed with the changes.

According to Tosca Spec 3.2.6.2
Whitespace: any number of spaces (including zero or none) SHALL be allowed between the scalar value and the unit value.

The pattern matching accounts for only 1 space. 

Also, the matcher class [a-z][A-Z]{1,3} would pass even for a string which might not fall under the "Recognized units" for scalar type (3.2.6.4.1, 3.2.6.5.1 and 3.2.6.6.1). 
e.g 10 abc
May be this method can be used in the validato. Looks more in line with the spec.

Hi Ojas, I have changed the regex to allow spaces between the scalar value and the unit value. 

This regex is used to check if the String structure matches with the Tosca Scalar Unit Types structure. There is another method that validates if the given String has a Recognized Tosca unit.
I have moved it to the ScalarUnitValidator class.
